general: For those born in the Year of the Dragon, the Year of the Horse brings a relatively stable fortune with no major clashes or conflicts. However, the power of auspicious stars remains weak, and the year is influenced by inauspicious stars. Therefore, careful planning and mental preparation are essential in all endeavors. Only the auspicious star "Tian Jie" appears in the Year of the Horse's fortune, symbolizing the ability to turn danger into safety and achieve positive results when encountering difficulties. This means that while problems will arise initially, solutions will be found. Thus, many setbacks are inevitable in the Year of the Horse, but these will ultimately be overcome. It is advised that those born in the Year of the Dragon not set overly ambitious goals but adopt a more relaxed, "Buddhist" mindset. Strive for excellence without excessive concern for gains and losses; this may lead to unexpected surprises. "Tian Jie" also signifies "relief" in relationships. Therefore, engaged couples should ensure clear division of roles, communicate effectively, and be tolerant of each other when planning their wedding to avoid arguments over trivial matters that could lead to a breakup. Although "Tianjie" (天解) has some negative impact on romantic relationships, it can resolve misunderstandings and eliminate past grievances in interpersonal relationships. If there have been misunderstandings and estrangement between old friends, there is a chance to rekindle old friendships. Regarding inauspicious stars, "Tiangou" (天狗) and "Diaoke" (吊客) will be present in the Year of the Horse, meaning that you are prone to minor accidents and frights when traveling. Fortunately, "Tianjie" can mitigate some of the dangers. However, you should still pay attention to safety while traveling and try to avoid high-risk outdoor activities such as mountain climbing, rock climbing, skiing, or diving. If you must participate, go with companions and purchase travel insurance for protection. Furthermore, these inauspicious stars will also affect family fortune. Those born in the Year of the Dragon need to pay attention to the health of elders in their family and accompany them to the doctor if they are unwell. You should try to avoid visiting the sick or attending funerals. If you must attend such events, it is best to wear some amulets to enhance your personal energy. "Widow Star" represents loneliness and coldness. According to traditional sayings, "Men fear the Lonely Star, women avoid the Widow Star." Women born in the Year of the Dragon will feel particularly lonely in the Year of the Horse. Even if they have partners, they may feel that their partners do not understand their inner feelings. They should communicate more and treat each other honestly. "Moon Sha" brings troubles to women. It is not advisable to invest or run a business with women in the Year of the Horse, as it is easy to cause enmity due to money issues. If the direct supervisor of an employee is female, they will face more unreasonable demands and need to handle them with high emotional intelligence. Overall, the fortune of the Year of the Horse is average. However, "Tian Jie" is not an absolutely auspicious star. Therefore, one should be mentally prepared to deal with possible troubles and obstacles. Things will not be easy to achieve, but fortunately, they can be resolved perfectly in the end. Therefore, it is necessary to make double preparations in advance to prevent unexpected situations from affecting the plan.

bornYear.reading: In the Year of the Earth Dragon, the elderly, despite their advanced age, were fortunate to maintain an active mindset and have enough energy and stamina to attend gatherings with family and friends, ensuring their lives remained joyful. Unfortunately, their digestive systems are relatively fragile, so they must pay special attention to maintaining a light and bland diet. Furthermore, they should be mindful of their sleeping environment, ensuring it is not disturbed by light or noise. It is suggested that placing heavy copper objects or gourds near their beds can help dispel negative energy and improve sleep quality.

The Year of the Horse will be a year of frequent gatherings and activities, with opportunities for short trips, making life quite fulfilling. If you have tracheal or nasal allergies, you should be especially careful in the Year of the Horse to avoid worsening your condition. At the same time, in this year of clash, interpersonal relationships must be carefully managed; harmony should be prioritized, and conflicts should be avoided as much as possible. I hope my studies will progress smoothly, and that I will maintain a strong curiosity about new discoveries in the world around me, exploring new knowledge and skills in multiple fields to broaden my horizons.
